questions.question: What single element did Julia add to her proposals that increased her close rate from 18% to 34%?

options.text: A 'Your ROI' section with the prospect's specific numbers from discovery

questions.explanation: Julia cut her proposal from 15 pages to 3 and added a personalized ROI section using numbers from the discovery call. Proposals close with clarity of value and specificity of outcome, not volume of information.

questions.question: Solo founders who document their discovery, proposal, and closing processes close what percentage more deals?
questions.explanation: Solo founders with documented conversion processes close 28% more deals than those who wing it. Building the document forces you to think through every step, identify failures, and pre-solve objections.

questions.question: How quickly should a proposal be sent after a discovery call?

options.text: After the prospect follows up to request it

questions.explanation: Proposals should go out within 24 hours of the discovery call. This is non-negotiable. Delays allow enthusiasm to cool and second thoughts to form. If you can't send in 24 hours, send a placeholder email with a timeline.

questions.question: When adapting to a D-style (Dominant) buyer, you should spend extra time building rapport before getting to outcomes.
questions.explanation: False. D-style buyers are direct, fast, and results-focused. Skip the rapport-building and get to outcomes immediately. They want a one-page executive summary with specific ROI numbers, not a long relationship-building conversation.

questions.question: Review your last 3-5 proposals. What was your proposal-to-close rate? What patterns do you see in lost proposals? What one change would most improve your conversion?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response includes actual proposal-to-close data, identifies specific patterns in lost deals (pricing, timing, ghosting), and proposes one concrete improvement such as adding an ROI section, shortening the proposal, or improving follow-up cadence.
